translations.descricao: Parkinson's disease (PD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disease characterized by tremor, rigidity, bradykinesia, and postural instability. The diagnosis is mainly clinical, without routine population screening recommended. Management involves physical, pharmacological, and nutritional therapy to improve quality of life.
translations.sus.indicacao: There is no specific population screening program for PD in SUS; diagnosis based on symptoms and neurological evaluation in primary care. Multidisciplinary management is recommended for confirmed cases.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adults with suggestive symptoms (resting tremor, bradykinesia) ≥50 years; elderly with motor decline.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The American Physical Therapy Association (2022) recommends physical therapy for PD management. Canadian Guideline (2019) emphasizes early diagnosis and pharmacological treatment. Consensus on tremors (2018) aids in classification.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: Global prevalence of PD is approximately 1% in individuals >60 years, affecting about 10 million people. In Canada and similar contexts, 0.3% in the general population. In Brazil, estimates indicate 200,000 cases, with prevalence of 0.5-1% in the elderly.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Annual incidence of 8-18 cases per 100,000 inhabitants, increasing with age.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: PD contributes to mortality due to complications such as falls and pneumonia; standardized mortality rate ~1.5 times higher than the general population.
